perturbation.original_text: 1.2. SPONSOR PLACEMENTS. During the Term, in no way limiting the foregoing in Section 1.1, Sponsor will receive the following sponsorship and promotional placements on the drkoop.com Website: (i) Sponsor shall be the * * * sponsor of the Nutrition Center on the drkoop.com Website and each area (other than the "Daily Special" area, the "Healthy Recipes" area and any other area which may be created in the future which specifically relates to cooking or food recipes (collectively, the "Excluded Areas")) within the Nutrition Center, including, the "Vitamins & Supplements" area, the "Vitamins and Minerals" area, the "Nutrition News" area, the "Nutrition for Healthy Living" area and the "Nutrition for your Condition" area (collectively, the "Sponsor Areas"). * * * (ii) * * * (iii) From time to time, drkoop.com shall create content which features vitamins and nutritional supplements. Sponsor's Advertising Content shall be displayed on such pages which host vitamins and nutritional supplement content to the same extent and subject to the same restrictions as such Sponsor Advertising Content is displayed in the Sponsor Areas. (iv) * * * Drkoop.com's obligations with respect to each area of the drkoop.com Website set forth in this Section 1.2 shall also apply to all areas which are successors or replacements to such areas and to all new vitamin and nutrition areas on the drkoop.com Website launched on the drkoop.com Website after the date of this Agreement. * * * Sponsor may promote the sale of vitamins and supplements in the Sponsor Areas.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2. SPONSOR PLACEMENTS. During the Term, in no way limiting the foregoing in Section 1.1, Sponsor will receive sponsorship and promotional placements on the drkoop.com Website as drkoop.com deems appropriate. Sponsor placements are at the sole discretion of DrKoop.com. Sponsor may promote the sale of vitamins and supplements in areas approved by drkoop.com.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies the Sponsor's placements within the Nutrition Center of the drkoop.com Website, including specific areas like "Vitamins & Supplements". The changed text removes all specifics and states that sponsor placements are at the sole discretion of DrKoop.com, creating an in-text contradiction with the earlier, more defined terms. This contradicts the promise of defined placement in the sponsor area, creating uncertainty about what rights the sponsor actually has. Further Sections reffering to Sponsor placement, like Section 2.1, are now potentially contradicting due to this perturbation, as they still reffered to defined placements, which placement is now undefined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.1. CONTENT. For each of the placements described in Section 1, including all banner advertisements and e-commerce tiles, Sponsor shall provide drkoop.com with all content including all trademarks, logos or banners (the "Sponsor Advertising Content"), in accordance with the specifications set forth on Exhibit C attached hereto, which will be displayed on the drkoop.com Website and which will link, in Sponsor's discretion, to either the Sponsor Site or Vitamin Buzz. The parties hereto agree to cooperate and work together in the establishment of all links, buttons and banners placed pursuant to this Agreement. Links from one party's Site to the other party's Site shall in no way alter the look, feel or functionality of the linked Site.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the terms of Sponsor placement, stating it is now at DrKoop.com's discretion, while the contradicted text (Section 2.1) refers to specific placements described in Section 1, which is the section that was modified. This creates a direct contradiction as Section 2.1 assumes predefined placements that no longer exist under the changed Section 1.2. The sponsor Advertising Content is to be displayed based on placements in Section 1, which no longer has a standard of placement, but rather, the discretion of DrKoop.com.

perturbation.original_text: 2.3. STATISTICS. Drkoop.com shall provide Sponsor with Sponsor usage reports on a monthly basis. Sponsor shall have the right to use such data for its internal business purposes, but may not provide such data for use by third parties. Such reports shall contain substantially the same types of information delivered to other of drkoop.com's similarly situated partners, which reports will include information regarding impressions, clickthroughs and any information known about the users of such areas in aggregate form.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.3. STATISTICS. Drkoop.com may provide Sponsor with Sponsor usage reports. The frequency, content, and use of these reports will be determined at drkoop.com's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates monthly usage reports with specified content (impressions, clickthroughs, user data). The changed text makes the provision of these reports discretionary, removing the guarantee of consistent information. This contradicts any business planning made by Sponsor on the expectation of a report, creating uncertainty for business strategies. By changing 'shall' to 'may', with no certainty of the reports being made, affects further sections relying on this data. For example section 5.4
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.4. SPONSOR ADVERTISING CONTENT. drkoop.com hereby confirms and agrees that during the Term Sponsor shall be able to serve up its own advertising using NetGravity software and tags, and that drkoop.com shall not do anything which would interfere or hamper such serving. Notwithstanding anything in this Agreement, all information regarding Sponsor Advertising Content (including Sponsor banner advertisements and e-commerce tiles), including all users viewing and clicking information with respect thereto, shall be deemed to be Confidential Information of Sponsor (collectively, "Sponsor Confidential Advertising Information"). To the extent that in connection with drkoop.com's advertising efforts, or otherwise, any third party may or will receive any Sponsor Confidential Advertising Information from or through drkoop.com, drkoop.com agrees that prior to such third party receiving any such information drkoop.com will enter into an agreement with such third party pursuant to which such third party will agree to keep any such Sponsor Confidential Advertising Information received by such third party confidential to the same extent as drkoop.com is required to keep such information confidential under the Agreement. To the extent that any third party breaches any such agreement of confidentiality with drkoop.com, drkoop.com hereby agrees to enforce its rights and pursue its remedies under such agreement to the fullest extent permitted by law, including seeking equitable relief, * * *.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates monthly usage reports with specified content. The changed text makes providing these reports discretionary, undermining the sponsor's ability to track advertising content, users, and click information, which under section 5.4, 'shall be deemed to be Confidential Information of Sponsor'. By making the reports discretionary, the sponsor has no guarantee of receiving this 'Confidential Information', directly contradicting the obligation established in section 5.4.

perturbation.original_text: 8.1. TERM; TERMINATION. 8.1.1. The initial term (the "Initial Term"; and together with all extensions and renewals, the "Term") will begin on the date set forth above * * * (the "Launch Date") on which: (i) each of the Sponsor Areas of the drkoop.com Website are operational in accordance with the terms of this Agreement (other than the e-commerce tile placements); and (ii) the links to the ____________________ * * * Certain information on this page has been omitted and filed separately with the Securities and Exchange Commission. Confidential treatment has been requested with respect to the omitted portions. 9 Sponsor Website or Vitamin Buzz contained in the Sponsor logos or the Sponsor banner advertisements are established in accordance with the terms of this Agreement, subject to earlier termination as set forth in this Agreement. If the Launch Date has not occurred by August 31, 1999, Sponsor shall, in its sole discretion, be entitled to terminate this Agreement without any liability and receive a full refund of all amounts paid by Sponsor to drkoop.com pursuant to this Agreement prior to the date of such termination. * * *
perturbation.changed_text: 8.1. TERM; TERMINATION. 8.1.1. The initial term (the "Initial Term"; and together with all extensions and renewals, the "Term") will begin on a date determined solely by drkoop.com. The term can be terminated at any time with a 30 day notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines the 'Launch Date' as the start of the term, conditioned on sponsor areas being operational. The changed text removes any reference to sponsor area operability and removes the August 31, 1999, termination option and full refund. This undermines Section 1.2 in particular, which defines sponsor placements. The original Section 8.1 is explicit in what the sponsor is entitled to if the launch date is missed, and with the modified section the Sponsor has no protection should the agreement not launch. This is an in-text contradiction because the entire premise of the agreement is to set up a sponsor area. Affects sections reffering to payments like section 2.5.1
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.5.1. FEES. The fee for the placements and other rights provided under this Agreement for the Initial Term (as defined below) is * * * is payable within * * * of the date of this Agreement, with the balance of such fee payable by Sponsor in * * * consecutive equal installments of * * * payable by the * * * of the Initial Term commencing * * * following * * * the Launch Date (as defined below).
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 8.1 removes the specific conditions (sponsor areas operational by a certain date) under which the initial term begins and replaces it with a vague determination solely by drkoop.com. It also eliminates the Sponsor's right to terminate the agreement and receive a full refund if the launch date is missed. This directly contradicts Section 2.5.1, which bases the payment schedule on the 'Launch Date' as defined in the original Section 8.1. The changed text effectively removes the 'Launch Date' as a defined term and the Sponsor's protection, creating a clear conflict regarding payment obligations and the conditions for the agreement's commencement. The initial term is now conditional only on drkoop.com, leaving the sponsor without a recourse if the launch is indefinitely delayed. Therefore, the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction.
